rationale: The revision closes the named bd/list/ancestry and R3 plan/metadata fail-open legs, preserves the advisory wrapper contract, scopes refusal to the finalizing epic, keeps hatches non-bypassing, and honestly discloses the raw-merge and blp6 residuals. One fail-open residue remains in R1: branch existence is a load-bearing detection step, but lifecycle currently uses gitutil.BranchExists(name) bool, which maps every `git rev-parse --verify` error to false. Thus a branch-probe infrastructure error is indistinguishable from a cleanly deleted branch. For an ordinary unresolved RC there need be no R3 obligation; the gate can pass, then FinalizeEpic's independent worktree loop can see and merge the branch. This contradicts R1's `refuses when it cannot prove otherwise` and `any infra error` posture. The error-preserving core must preserve branch-probe errors too, while the legacy wrapper continues converting them to its existing fail-open result.
concrete_changes_required: Make the ScanOrphanedClosedBeads branch-existence probe error-preserving (for example, add a `(bool, error)` git/ref seam) and make impl approve refuse on that error; keep FindOrphanedClosedBeads behavior unchanged by swallowing it in the wrapper.

Extend AC1 and lifecycle/approve fault-injection tests to prove a branch-existence probe error refuses before epic close, phase writes, FinalizeEpic, merge, or push, while the legacy wrapper remains fail-open.

findings.note: Named epic lookup, bd-list, and ancestry failures are fail-closed, but BranchExists still erases git probe errors into false, leaving an o4fd-class pass-to-FinalizeEpic path.
